data.title: EQT Life Sciences leads EUR 51M Series B in Exciva for Alzheimer's therapy, advancing Deraphan into Phase 2 trial.
data.description: Exciva has secured Series B financing to back the Phase 2 clinical trial of Deraphan, its primary candidate for treating agitation linked to Alzheimer's disease. The funding round was jointly led by EQT Life Sciences from its LSP Dementia Fund, and Gimv, with investment from Fountain Healthcare.

data.title: Leonardo.Ai unveils guide on Nano Banana AI models, with tips on image editing, character consistency, and 4K production.
data.description: Leonardo.Ai has introduced comprehensive prompting workflows specifically designed for Google's Nano Banana AI models. These workflows provide detailed guidance on various aspects including image editing, ensuring character consistency, and utilizing 4K production techniques. This release aims to enhance the performance and capabilities of the AI models for users. This development is set to impact a wide range of industries and professionals who rely on AI technology for their work.

data.title: Investor Tom Nash sees 7x potential growth in Bloom Energy Corp., projecting 1,400% revenue growth in 5 years if executed.
data.description: Tom Nash, a prominent investor, has identified a California-based energy company as a key player in resolving AI's significant issue of reliable power for data centers. Nash, well-known for his optimistic predictions on Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR) and Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA), is now endorsing fuel-cell producer Bloom Energy Corp. (NYSE: BE) as his latest substantial investment opportunity. Nash anticipates a staggering 7x increase in value by 2030 for this company. Through his YouTube channel, Nash emphasized how Bloom Energy's on-site fuel-cell systems can alleviate the pressure on the strained U.S. electrical grid for data centers, describing it as a groundbreaking solution for the AI sector. By installing these systems in parking lots, data centers can operate independently from the grid. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
